title: Stellar Wave 3 — Operation mapping and policy context
goal: Add a mapping layer that translates decoded Stellar operations into the existing brain's vocabulary: a static operation->actionKind+riskClass table and a policy-context derivation function (amount_usd, recipient_address, recipient_known) extended additively with Stellar flags (changes_trustline, changes_signers). The brain stays untouched; this wave only feeds the existing policy engine the right inputs so the four decision-only demo cases resolve correctly. Solana stays unchanged.

openQuestions.question: What is the default disposition for an unmapped or future Stellar operation type that is not in the table?
openQuestions.resolution: DECIDED: Fail-closed. Unmapped/future operation types map to riskClass BLOCKED_UNKNOWN with critical:true, forcing REQUIRE_HUMAN_APPROVAL (ESCALATE) rather than ALLOW. Never silently pass an unknown operation.

openQuestions.question: Should the Stellar operation->actionKind table live in code or in policy configuration?
openQuestions.resolution: DECIDED: In code for the MVP (a static table in stellarOperationMap.ts), so the mapping is testable and reviewable without config plumbing. Making the table configurable via policy config is deferred to a later wave.

acceptanceCriteria: Every Stellar operation type maps to a defined actionKind and riskClass; there is no operation type for which the map is silent.
payment, pathPaymentStrictSend, and pathPaymentStrictReceive map to actionKind 'transfer' with riskClass SENSITIVE_EXECUTION.
changeTrust is marked critical, sets the additive flag changes_trustline:true, and forces REQUIRE_HUMAN_APPROVAL (ESCALATE).
setOptions is marked critical, sets the additive flag changes_signers:true, and forces REQUIRE_HUMAN_APPROVAL (ESCALATE).
createAccount maps to a transfer-like actionKind; manageData, manageSellOffer, manageBuyOffer, and other types map to the nearest actionKind with conservative escalate-by-default handling.
New context flags are additive: they extend PolicyEvaluationContext.flags without removing, renaming, or breaking any existing field or existing policy evaluation.
In a multi-operation envelope, if any operation is critical the whole envelope escalates.
An unmapped or future operation type defaults fail-closed to BLOCKED_UNKNOWN and escalation.

The four decision-only demo cases (ALLOW within policy, DENY on non-allowlisted destination, ESCALATE on amount out of range, ESCALATE on setOptions/changeTrust present) pass end-to-end using the UNCHANGED policy engine.
The brain (policy engine, LLM judge, sanitizer, COMPASS_DECISIONS) is untouched and Solana stays green.
nonGoals: No change to the policy engine, LLM judge, sanitizer, COMPASS_DECISIONS contract, or MCP proxy decision logic (brain stays untouched).
No new decision types; reuse existing COMPASS_DECISIONS.
No removal or renaming of existing PolicyEvaluationContext fields (flags are additive only).
No changes to any Solana provider or Solana behavior.
No signing, submission, simulation, or co-signer/threshold logic (later waves).
No XDR decoding (Wave 2 owns that; Wave 3 consumes its output).
No mainnet support of any kind.

tasks.title: Add RED tests for the Stellar operation map
tasks.description: Write failing tests asserting that mapStellarOperation returns the expected actionKind + riskClass for every known operation type, that changeTrust and setOptions are critical with their additive flags, and that unmapped types fail closed to BLOCKED_UNKNOWN + critical.
testsSpec: payment, pathPaymentStrictSend, pathPaymentStrictReceive -> actionKind 'transfer', riskClass SENSITIVE_EXECUTION, critical:false
manageData, manageSellOffer, manageBuyOffer -> mapped with conservative critical:true handling
an unmapped/future operation type -> riskClass BLOCKED_UNKNOWN, critical:true (fail-closed)
No legacy/ import appears in any new Wave 3 file

tasks.title: Implement stellarOperationMap.ts
tasks.description: Create the static operation->actionKind+riskClass table with critical flags and additive context flags, plus mapStellarOperation with a fail-closed default. Reuse TOOL_RISK_CLASSES from the shared contracts; introduce no new decision types.

tasks.title: Add RED tests for Stellar policy-context derivation
tasks.description: Write failing tests for deriveStellarPolicyContext: single-payment context fields, additive flag merging, and multi-operation escalation when any operation is critical.
testsSpec: single payment -> context with amount_usd, recipient_address, recipient_known derived from the decoded operation
changeTrust -> context.flags.changes_trustline:true with pre-existing flags preserved
setOptions -> context.flags.changes_signers:true with pre-existing flags preserved
envelope with one critical operation plus benign ones -> context drives escalation
existing flags (unknown_program, unlimited_delegate, authority_change, suspicious_recipient) are never removed or renamed

tasks.title: Implement stellarPolicyContext.ts
tasks.description: Create deriveStellarPolicyContext that aggregates decoded operations into the existing PolicyEvaluationContext shape, additively merges the changes_trustline / changes_signers flags, and escalates the whole envelope if any operation is critical. Read-only against Wave 2 decoded operations.

tasks.title: Add decision-only end-to-end tests against the unchanged policy engine
tasks.description: Wire the mapping outputs into the existing policy engine in tests and assert the four demo cases resolve correctly without modifying the brain.
testsSpec: legit payment within policy to allowlisted recipient -> ALLOW
payment amount out of range -> REQUIRE_HUMAN_APPROVAL (ESCALATE)
envelope containing setOptions or changeTrust -> REQUIRE_HUMAN_APPROVAL (ESCALATE)

tasks.title: Validate Wave 3 and pause for review
tasks.description: Run the full validation suite and confirm Solana stays green and the brain is untouched before review.
